title: Unix sockets can't be checkpoint exported
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

UNIX domain sockets (special type of file) can't be tar:ed when exporting a checkpoint. I think it's a limitation in go's tar package, so maybe no quick fix here.. 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run -d docker.io/alpine sh -c "apk add netcat-openbsd && exec nc -lU /tmp/sock"`
2. `sleep 10 # wait some time for the pkg to be installed`
3. `podman container checkpoint --latest --export dump.tarz `

**Describe the results you received:**

```
ERRO[0000] Can't add file /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/1b0bb322728fe44edc410f71bdbb2e51030a64270c54d1bb48a4d3bb9fe81029/merged//tmp/sock to tar: archive/tar: sockets not supported
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

The checkpoint to succeed without errors.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.0
API Version:  3.4.0
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Thu Sep 30 21:40:21 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

comments.body: @adrianreber PTAL. I don't think there's much we can do about this except ignore sockets?

Exactly. The tar implementation in Go is the problem.

A workaround is to tell podman to mount a tmpfs at that location. Then CRIU will tar up the tmpfs in the checkpoint using the tar from the OS and the socket can be checkpointed.
Thank you, yes, discovered that, used /dev/shm. Abstract Unix sockets works well too (path that starts with `@`). Closing this issue.
